response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack and web-focused senior developer, but the role requires native-level Japanese, deep C/UNIX embedded firmware expertise, Linux kernel modification experience, and L2/L3 networking and security protocol knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP). The resume shows no Japanese language ability, no embedded firmware or LAN-switch experience, only brief historical C/C++ exposure at National Instruments (~6 months), and no demonstrated work on kernel internals, security protocols, or switch chip SDKs.
strengths: 15+ years of professional software development across diverse stacks
Some prior C/C++ experience at National Instruments on developer tooling
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing, craftsmanship mindset
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed (hard requirement is native-level)
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, or Linux kernel modification experience
No exposure to required security/networking stack (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP, switch chips)

key_factors.factor: No security or L2/L3 networking experience
